title: vernonia dura var. firmula
intro: Vernonia dura var. firmula, also known as Firmula, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to tropical regions of Asia and Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and wounds. Firmula has been shown to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. In traditional medicine, Firmula is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are prepared by drying the plant's leaves and roots, then steeping them in hot water or mixing with other herbs.
